## Summary

Implements double-click inline editing for node labels in the canvas visualization, allowing users to edit the description field directly from the canvas without opening the properties panel.

Fixes #952

## Changes Made

### Core Implementation
- Added inline editing capability to `CustomNodeLabel` component
- Implemented double-click handler to enter edit mode
- Added keyboard controls: Enter to save, Escape to cancel
- Integrated with existing node model update mechanism via `updateModel`

### Edge Case Handling
- Added guard to check `vizNode` and `onDescriptionChange` exist before allowing editing
- Fixed Escape key cancellation with `isCancelling` state to prevent blur event from saving
- Added whitespace trimming when saving descriptions
- Updated React hook dependency arrays for proper behavior

### Accessibility
- Added `aria-label` attribute to input field for screen reader support

## Testing

### Unit Tests (7/7 passing)
- Basic double-click editing functionality
- Whitespace trimming verification
- Escape key cancellation without saving
- Input field appearance and interaction
- Model update integration

### End-to-End Testing
Manually verified the following scenarios:
1. Double-click on node label opens inline editor
2. Typing and pressing Enter saves the description
3. Pressing Escape cancels without saving
4. Clicking outside (blur) saves the changes
5. Whitespace is properly trimmed from input
6. Feature works across different node types (timer, log, etc.)

## Files Modified

- `packages/ui/src/components/Visualization/Custom/Node/CustomNode.tsx` - Implementation
- `packages/ui/src/components/Visualization/Custom/Node/CustomNode.test.tsx` - Test coverage

## Additional Notes

- All existing tests continue to pass
- No breaking changes introduced
- Feature is backward compatible
- Follows existing code patterns and conventions

Introduces `MappingValidationService` to centralise mapping pair validation rules, replacing `VisualizationService testNodePair()`. `DnDHandler.handleDragEnd()` now returns a `DnDResult` to propagate error message if there is. - Invalid cross-side drops (unselected xs:choice, container/terminal mismatch) show an error toast - Hovering over an invalid target shows a disabled border and field name style - Hovering over a same-side droppable (source→source, target→target) is disabled at DNDKit level, i.e. no visual feedback on hover, silently ignored if dropped https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fe42c6c6-4dd8-4b13-ad14-1c5123a71775 Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2814 - Auto-generate choose-when-otherwise mapping when DnD is performed from a choice source to a target field - MappingValidationService: Skip regular container<>terminal check in `validateContainerRules()` for choice node mapping where `validateChoiceRules()` validates the choice specific rules Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2814 Handle the case when the source choice is already selected Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2731 - Instead of flatten all xs:choice members, create a choice field and wrap members as children, making the artificial choice field visible in the document tree - Skip choice field on XPath generation - Introduce createNodeTitle() and getChoiceMemberLabel() to generate choice member display label with numbered suffixes (choice1, choice2, …) when multiple nested choice wrappers appear as siblings ### step choice node from camel-spring.xsd ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Drag-and-drop now returns explicit success/failure results, surfaces danger alerts on failures, and improves drag-preview titles for mapped nodes; choice label text rendered italic; drag preview ignores pointer events. * **Documentation** * Expanded JSDoc for visualization and node models. * **Tests** * Added extensive DnD, mapping-validation, visualization, schema, XPath, and unit tests. * **Chores** * Removed deprecated choice-display helper.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2989

The core design change is replacing the eagerly-populated `originalType`/`originalTypeQName` fields on `IField` with a lazily-captured `originalField?: IOriginalFieldState` snapshot. Previously, every field carried redundant copies of its own type set at document-parse time; now the snapshot is taken only on first expansion (`resolveTypeFragment`) or just before the first override is applied, and cleared when the override is reverted. This eliminates build-time data duplication and makes the snapshot a true runtime record of pre-override state.

Alongside this:
 - `SUBSTITUTION` variant added to `FieldOverrideVariant` (renamed from `TypeOverrideVariant` for accuracy)
 - `IFieldSubstitution` metadata interface introduced and plumbed through `IDocumentMetadata` / `DocumentDefinition`

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2995

- By capturing original field snapshot to `originalField: IOriginalFieldState` on `IFleld`, `restoreOriginalTypeToField` restores both `fields[]` and `namedTypeFragmentRef` when Field Override is reverted

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2990

Add low level API `getSubstitutionCandidates()` to retrieve field substitution candidates from the available schema for the document

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2991

- High level API addition in FieldTypeOverrideService:
  - getFieldSubstitutionCandidates()
  - applyFieldSubstitution()
  - revertFieldSubstitution()
- Subsequent logic:
  - New: XmlSchemaTypesService.resolveSubstitution()
  - Update: DocumentUtilService.processOverrides() - added field substitution handling
  - New: SchemaPathService.buildOriginal() - reconstruct the HEAD element's schema path from field.originalField after substitution has changed the live field name
  - New: XmlSchemaDocumentUtilService.buildElementFragmentKey() - extracted as a shared helper for the `__elem:nsURI:localPart` fragment key pattern
  - New: IFieldSubstituteInfo interface - represents resolved substitute element's wire name and type metadata

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2989

The core design change is replacing the eagerly-populated `originalType`/`originalTypeQName` fields on `IField` with a lazily-captured `originalField?: IOriginalFieldState` snapshot. Previously, every field carried redundant copies of its own type set at document-parse time; now the snapshot is taken only on first expansion (`resolveTypeFragment`) or just before the first override is applied, and cleared when the override is reverted. This eliminates build-time data duplication and makes the snapshot a true runtime record of pre-override state.

Alongside this:
 - `SUBSTITUTION` variant added to `FieldOverrideVariant` (renamed from `TypeOverrideVariant` for accuracy)
 - `IFieldSubstitution` metadata interface introduced and plumbed through `IDocumentMetadata` / `DocumentDefinition`

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2995

- By capturing original field snapshot to `originalField: IOriginalFieldState` on `IFleld`, `restoreOriginalTypeToField` restores both `fields[]` and `namedTypeFragmentRef` when Field Override is reverted

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary
- Add `ChoiceSelectionService` — orchestrator for xs:choice selection operations in the DataMapper
- Provides `applyChoiceSelections` (restore on load), `setChoiceSelection`, `clearChoiceSelection`, `buildChoicePath`, `resolveChoicePath`
- Depth-ordered application ensures parent choices are applied before children
- Graceful degradation with `console.warn` for invalid paths/indices on load
- Cascade invalidation removes descendant selections when parent changes
- Follows existing facade pattern (parallel to `FieldTypeOverrideService`)

## Test Coverage
- 31 tests covering all code paths
- Path building/resolution (top-level, nested, sibling, invalid)
- Set selection (valid, boundary index 0, definition sync, errors, cascade invalidation)
- Clear selection (valid, already cleared, errors, cascade invalidation)
- Apply selections (single, multiple, depth ordering, invalid paths, invalid indices, partial, empty)
- Integration (set → persist → reload → clear, multiple nested selections)

## Pre-Landing Review
No issues found.

## Test plan
- [x] All 31 choice-selection service tests pass
- [x] All 186 related tests (schema-path, document-util) pass — no regressions

pull_requests_reviewed.body: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/pull/3090 needs to go in before this one Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2840 --- # UI mockup for xsl:variable support This demonstrates the proposed DataMapper new feature, `xsl:variable` support which allows user to create a scoped variable in the target document tree, create an interim value from other source field values, then use it as a source variable. ## Scenario the source has an Orders collection parameter. The target Invoice document has a Subtotals collection field wrapped with for-each ($Orders/Order). Inside the loop, $taxAmount captures the per-item tax so it can be reused in the Subtotal expression without duplicating the calculation. 1. **Add variable** — open the 3-dots context menu on the `for-each` node and choose `Add Variable….` 2. **Map source field → variable** — drag Price onto `$taxAmount`. The XPath expression is prefilled with `Price`; extend it to `Price * 0.1`. 3. **Map to Subtotal** — drag both `Price` and `$taxAmount` onto `Subtotal`. Make the expression to be `Price + $taxAmount`. --- ## Step 1: Add Variable The `for-each ($Orders/Order)` block is already defined under Subtotals. Open the 3-dots context menu on the for-each node and choose Add Variable…. An inline name input appears inside the for-each block. Type taxAmount and click ✓ to confirm. The input validates in real-time: must be a valid QName. Click ✕ to cancel. Once the variable is created, it appears in Variables section in the Source Panel. ## Step 2: Rename Variable After the variable is created, it can be renamed at any time. Open the 3-dots context menu on the `$taxAmount` variable node in the target tree and choose **Rename variable….** The variable name becomes an inline input pre-filled with the current name. Edit the name and click `✓` to confirm, or `✕` to cancel. The new name is reflected immediately in both the target tree and the source **Variables** section. ## Step 3: Map Source Field to Variable Drag `Price` from the `Orders` parameter onto the `$taxAmount` variable node. The XPath expression is pre-filled with `Price` (relative to the current loop item). The user extends it to `Price * 0.1`. ## Step 4: Map from Variable Drag `Price` and `$taxAmount` from the source panel onto `Subtotal`. Make the expression to be `Price + $taxAmount`. The tax is computed once per iteration via the variable and reused — no need to duplicate `Price * 0.1` inline. --- ## Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Variable-mapping mock UI with step-driven workflow, inline variable-name input (focus/select, validation, Enter/Escape, confirm/cancel). * **Documentation** * Added Storybook stories for variable workflows; several mockups include links to issue tracker entries. * **Style** * New stylesheet for variable mockup layout, panels, rows, inputs, and mapping visuals. * **User-visible Changes** * Variable labels now display without a leading "$" across UI, dialogs, and confirmations.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: chore(DataMapper): UI mockup for xsl:variable support

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2860 --- # UI mockup for xsl:for-each-group support This demonstrates the proposed DataMapper new feature, `xsl:for-each-group` support which allows user to configure `for-each-group` mapping supported by XSLT. ## Scenario Group a source collection of Item elements by Category using XSLT 2.0 xsl:for-each-group, then iterate over each group's items with for-each current-group() to map individual fields. 1. **Wrap with for-each-group** — open the 3-dots context menu on the Item collection field and choose Wrap with for-each-group. 2. **Configure grouping** — the modal opens automatically; select a grouping strategy (e.g. Group By) and enter the grouping XPath expression (e.g. Category). 3. **Set select expression** — fill in the select inline input on the for-each-group node (e.g. $cart/ns0:Cart/Item). Hover the label to inspect the configured strategy and expression at any time. 4. **Reconfigure if needed** — open the ⋮ menu on for-each-group and choose Configure grouping to reopen the modal pre-populated with the current values. 5. **Wrap with for-each current-group()** — inside the for-each-group, open the ⋮ menu on Item and choose Wrap with for-each current-group(). The select expression is set to current-group() automatically. 6. **Map child fields** — expand Item inside the inner for-each and map Title and Quantity to their XPath expressions. --- ## Step 1: Wrap a collection field with for-each-group Open the 3-dots context menu on the Item collection field in the target document tree. In addition to the existing wrapping options, a new **“Wrap with for-each-group”** item appears. Selecting it wraps the field and immediately opens the grouping configuration modal (Step 2). ## Step 2: Configure the grouping strategy Immediately after wrapping, the **Configure for-each-group** modal opens automatically. The user selects one of the four grouping strategies and enters the grouping XPath expression. “Group By” is pre-selected as the default. The select expression on the node itself is filled in separately via the inline text input after the modal is closed. ### Alternative idea exists * Instead of this modal way, we might be able to show `Grouping Strategy` artificial node as a child of `for-each-group` node, then make the `Grouping Strategy` expression always visible in the target document tree. ## Step 3: for-each-group node in the tree After saving the modal, the for-each-group node appears in the tree with the wrapped `Item` field as its child. The inline text input lets the user set the select expression. **Hover over the label** to see a tooltip summarising the current grouping configuration (strategy and expression), so the user can inspect the setup without reopening the modal. ## Step 4: Reconfigure via the 3-dot menu The 3-dots context menu on the for-each-group node exposes **“Configure grouping”** to reopen the configuration modal. Deletion is handled by the trash icon button next to the menu — consistent with how other mapping nodes are deleted in the tree. ## Step 5: Reconfigure grouping When the user clicks “Configure grouping”, the modal reopens pre-populated with the previously saved strategy and expression. The user can switch to a different strategy or update the grouping expression, then save to apply the changes. ## Step 6: Wrap Item with for-each current-group() Inside the for-each-group, the user opens the 3-dots context menu on the wrapped `Item` collection field. A new **“Wrap with for-each current-group()”** option appears. Selecting it wraps `Item` with a `for-each` whose select expression is automatically set to `current-group()` — a scoped XSLT function that iterates over the items in the current group. ## Step 7: Complete mapping structure The fully configured structure. `for-each-group` iterates over the source collection and groups items by the configured expression. Inside it, `for-each` `current-group()` iterates over each group's items, and the user maps individual fields (`Title`, `Quantity`) from the expanded Item node. The generated XSLT uses `xsl:for-each-group` with the chosen strategy attribute (e.g. group-by="Category"). --- ## Expected XSLT output ```xslt <xsl:value-of select="Title"/> ``` ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Added a UI mockup demonstrating the for-each-group data-mapper workflow across multiple phases with interactive states. * **Documentation** * Added seven Storybook stories showing step-by-step configuration, menus, modals, tooltips, and transitions. * Added an XSLT reference example illustrating four for-each-group strategies. * **Chores** * Storybook preview now forces a light theme for consistent visuals.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: chore(DataMapper): UI mockup for xsl:for-each-group support

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2855 --- # UI mock up for Container Mapping These stories demonstrate the proposed mapping creation behavior and mapping line styles that result from drag-and-dropping container fields in the DataMapper. Each scenario shows what the user sees after dropping a source container onto a target container. ### Collapsed vs. Expanded `partial` and `complete` line styles only appear at the container row when both sides are collapsed. When the user expands either side to the leaf level, individual child lines render as `regular` — the container-level distinction disappears and only the mapped vs unmapped children are visible. |Line type|Weight|When it appears| |--------------|----------|------------------------| |copy-of|boldest|DnD produced an exact namespace + structure match, or either side is xs:anyType| |complete|heavy|DnD auto-mapped all children (different namespaces), containers collapsed| |regular|normal|Any leaf-level field mapping, or containers expanded| |partial|thinnest|DnD matched only some children, containers collapsed| ## Partial auto-mapping — some children matched When drag and drop is performed between different container fields, and they have one or more same child(ren) but not all, it creates mappings for matching child(ren). When it is collapsed, thin line renders between parent field(s). When it is expanded to the children, it renders the line for each child mapping. ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Complete auto-mapping — all children matched When drag and drop is performed between different container fields, and both have exactly same children, It creates mappings for each children. When it is collapsed, heavier line renders between parent field(s). When it is expanded to the children, it renders the line for each child mapping. ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## xsl:copy-of - exact match When drag and drop is performed between exactly same fields (both namespace and local name matches), xsl:copy-of mapping is created and mapping line is drawn only between those fields (no line between children). ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## xsl:copy-of — xs:anyType involved When drag and drop is performed from or to xs:anyType field, xsl:copy-of mapping is created. ### Expected XSLT output #### Target is `xs:anyType` ```xslt ``` #### Source is `xs:anyType` ```xslt ``` ## Mapping line styles When a field is clicked, correlated mapping(s) is highlighted and mapping line colors change. --- ## Collection : exact match (xsl:copy-of) User drags Item (ns:a) [collection] onto Item (ns:a) [collection]. Same FQN → `xsl:copy-of` created. All repeating instances are copied at runtime. ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Collection : Parent doesn't match, but all children match (for-each) User drags Person (ns:a) [collection] onto Employee (ns:b) [collection]. Different FQN → xsl:for-each auto-created with select="/nsa:Root/nsa:Person". All leaf children (name, age, email) have matching names → all auto-mapped with value-of. Complete mapping. ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Collection : Parent doesn't match, children partially match (for-each) Same as complete, but target Employee (ns:b) has an extra employeeId field with no source counterpart. Only name, age, email are auto-mapped. employeeId is left unmapped (partial). ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Collection : Parent doesn't match, child complex field doesn't match (for-each) User drags Person (ns:a) [collection] (with address as leaf/string) onto Employee (ns:b) [collection] (with address as container with street/city children). name and age match (leaf→leaf) → auto-mapped. address exists on both sides but kind differs (leaf vs container) → skipped. Partial mapping. ### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Collection : Parent doesn't match, child complex field match (for-each) User drags Order (ns:a) [collection] (with id leaf + address (ns:a) container) onto Shipment (ns:b) [collection] (with trackingId leaf + address (ns:a) container). id vs trackingId → names don't match → unmapped. address (ns:a) on both sides → same FQN container → auto-mapped with copy-of. No recursive descent into address sub-children. #### Expected XSLT output ```xslt ``` ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Added visual mockups for DataMapper container mapping with interactive connector examples and demos (full and compact). * Introduced multiple link types and selected/unselected visual states for lines and endpoint dots. * Demonstrated partial/complete mappings, copy-of behaviors, and collection/for-each scenarios. * **Style** * Refined node action layout and button sizing for improved alignment and consistency.

The route template is a timer route which has timer parameters in `from`. In order to generate a direct route for REST operation, those timer parameter needs to be removed. Also use just `direct` for `uri` and put operationId in `parameters`, which is the Kaoto default style.

generated routes
```yaml
- route:
    id: route-updateUser
    from:
      id: direct-from-updateUser
      uri: direct
      parameters:
        name: updateUser
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: Operation updateUser not yet implemented
- route:
    id: route-deleteUser
    from:
      id: direct-from-deleteUser
      uri: direct
      parameters:
        name: deleteUser
      steps:
        - setBody:
            constant: Operation deleteUser not yet implemented
```

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/3002

 - Interactive elements without keyboard support (SourceDocumentNode, TargetDocumentNode, ExpansionPanel, InlineEdit): plain <div> and <span> elements with onClick handlers lacked role="button", tabIndex, and keyboard event handlers. Added onKeyDown (Enter/Space) and proper ARIA roles to make them accessible and compliant.

Added onMouseDown preventDefault() to suppress unwanted focus-on-click while preserving keyboard accessibility. The tag button in the catalog card looks same and works same.
 - ErrorBoundary stale state read: setState was reading this.state directly inside the updater, which is unreliable in concurrent rendering. Changed to the functional form setState(prev => ...).
 - Regex constructor calls (camel-to-table.adapter.ts): replaced RegExp(...) constructor calls with regex literals, which is the preferred, safer form.
 - readFileAsString silent rejection (read-file-as-string.ts): reject() was called without an error value, making the failure untraceable. Now passes an Error instance.
 - version.ts variable re-assignment: mutable let variables populated inside a try/catch were replaced with const IIFEs, each encapsulating its own try/catch, eliminating implicit re-assignment.
 - Remove file packages/ui/.claude/settings.local.json which was accidentally merged

### Context
Currently, there's an infinite rerender in the Datamapper due to a `useState()` call inside of a `useCallback()` depending on the variable being updated.

```typescript
  const reloadNodeReferences = useCallback(() => {
    setNodeReferenceMap(new Map(nodeReferenceMap));
    setNodeReferenceVersion((v) => v + 1);
  }, [nodeReferenceMap]);
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/690e85d5-527f-4e78-9d25-b2715eef2c7e

### Changes
As a temporary fix, we're moving to a function signature for the `useState` call. The reason why this is temporary is because #2954 removes the `DataMapperCanvasProvider` entirely, fixing the issue from the root.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
[WIP] This one possibly need some adjustment after https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/pull/2962 is merged.

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2989

The core design change is replacing the eagerly-populated `originalType`/`originalTypeQName` fields on `IField` with a lazily-captured `originalField?: IOriginalFieldState` snapshot. Previously, every field carried redundant copies of its own type set at document-parse time; now the snapshot is taken only on first expansion (`resolveTypeFragment`) or just before the first override is applied, and cleared when the override is reverted. This eliminates build-time data duplication and makes the snapshot a true runtime record of pre-override state.

Alongside this:
 - `SUBSTITUTION` variant added to `FieldOverrideVariant` (renamed from `TypeOverrideVariant` for accuracy)
 - `IFieldSubstitution` metadata interface introduced and plumbed through `IDocumentMetadata` / `DocumentDefinition`

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2995

- By capturing original field snapshot to `originalField: IOriginalFieldState` on `IFleld`, `restoreOriginalTypeToField` restores both `fields[]` and `namedTypeFragmentRef` when Field Override is reverted

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Summary
This PR adds support for inserting new entities at a specific position relative to an existing entity, rather than always using the default XML schema ordering. This enables features like duplicating routes to appear immediately after the original route.

### Key Changes
- Added optional `insertAfterEntityId` parameter to `CamelResource.addNewEntity()` interface and `CamelRouteResource` implementation
- Introduced `getInsertionIndex()` private method that:
  - Places new entities right after the specified entity ID when provided
  - Falls back to `EntityOrderingService.findInsertionIndex()` for default XML schema ordering when the parameter is not provided or the ID is not found
- Updated `useDuplicateStep` hook to pass the original entity's ID when duplicating routes, ensuring duplicates appear immediately after the source
- Added comprehensive test coverage for the new insertion behavior:
  - Inserting after a middle route
  - Inserting after the last route
  - Inserting among mixed entity types
  - Fallback behavior when ID is not found or not provided

### Notes

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
…e Override

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2973

- Establishes a shared IBaseOverride interface with a schemaPath field (renaming the old path) used by both IFieldTypeOverride and IChoiceSelection. The {choice:N} segment syntax in schema paths allows overrides and selections to address fields through xs:choice compositors unambiguously.
- Field navigation is consolidated into SchemaPathService.navigateToField(), removing duplicated traversal logic scattered across DocumentUtilService and FieldTypeOverrideService. The previously separate processTypeOverrides() and processChoiceSelections() calls are replaced by a unified processOverrides() that applies them in depth order — type overrides before choice selections at the same depth — which is necessary for correct xs:choice resolution.
- A new invalidateDescendants() ensures that when a type override or choice selection changes, stale descendant overrides and selections are automatically removed, preventing inconsistent document state.
- Individual setFieldTypeOverride/removeFieldTypeOverride methods are replaced by bulk setFieldTypeOverrides() and setChoiceSelections(), which replace the entire array atomically, matching the pattern used for persistence.
- Finally, a bug is fixed where fieldTypeOverrides and choiceSelections were silently discarded on every document definition update because doCreateDocumentMetadata read them from a dead existingMetadata parameter that no caller ever passed — they are now correctly read from the DocumentDefinition itself.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Refactoring**
  * Switched public model to use schema-paths (schemaPath) instead of XPath-style path for addressing fields and choices.
  * Unified processing of type overrides and choice selections into a single, depth-ordered workflow that invalidates descendant entries and applies schema-path navigation.
  * Changed metadata persistence to replace override/selection sets in bulk for consistent updates and added a consolidated metadata shape.

* **Tests**
  * Expanded coverage for persistence, retrieval, replacement semantics, invalidation, and choice/navigation scenarios.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2977

When a schema attribute defines its type inline via a child <xs:simpleType> element rather than a type="..." attribute reference, the schema parser could not resolve a type name and crashed. The fix adds null-safety for this case, gracefully falling back to a generic AnyType rather than throwing.

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2978

When the same global element (with an anonymous complex type) was referenced from many different parent contexts, the schema parser expanded its full subtree independently for each reference, causing exponential object growth. The fix stores a global element's anonymous type as a named fragment just like named types.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

  * Added performance and correctness tests for a large, deeply nested XML schema with time-budget validation.

* **Chores**

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
- Adds a `repository_dispatch` step at the end of the `container-image-release` job in the release pipeline
- When a stable release is published, the workflow sends a `kaoto-release` event to `KaotoIO/kaoto-operator` with the released version as payload
- Only fires when `stable` is true (same condition as the stable image push)

The operator release process depends on the Kaoto application being released first. Today this coordination is entirely manual - someone has to remember to sync the community operator forks and trigger the operator release. This change starts an automated chain: once Kaoto is released, the operator repo is notified and can prepare for its own release automatically.

Requirements:

The `KAOTO_CI_TOKEN` secret must be available in this repository with `repo` scope on `KaotoIO/kaoto-operator` (see token setup below) @lhein 

This is in the context of #815

pull_requests_reviewed.body: ## Endpoint Properties Multi-Value Parameter Support This PR introduces a new custom field component for handling endpoint properties with multi-value parameters in Camel component configurations. ### Key Changes - **New `EndpointPropertiesField` component**: Custom form field that provides a toggle between table and key-value editor modes for endpoint properties configuration - **Multi-value parameter handling**: Added `MultiValueProperty.service` to serialize/deserialize multi-value parameters (e.g., `job.*`, `trigger.*` prefixes) between nested and flat formats - **Simplified model updates**: Removed automatic multi-value serialization from `AbstractCamelVisualEntity.updateModel()` - now handled at the form field level - **Schema enhancements**: Endpoint properties schema now includes `x-component-name` metadata for component-specific parameter handling - **Custom field factory**: Registered `EndpointPropertiesField` for schemas with title "Endpoint Properties" ### Technical Details - Multi-value parameters are now managed by the form field component rather than the model layer - The new field supports both structured (nested object) and flat (prefixed key) representations - Includes comprehensive test coverage for the new components and services - Maintains backward compatibility with existing endpoint configurations ### Files Changed - Added: `EndpointPropertiesField` component with table/editor toggle UI - Added: `MultiValueProperty.service` for parameter transformation - Modified: `camel-component-schema.service` to remove model-level multi-value handling - Modified: `abstract-camel-visual-entity` to simplify `updateModel()` logic - Updated: Custom fields factory to register the new field type fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2968 fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2543 ## Summary by CodeRabbit ## Release Notes * **New Features** * Added Endpoint Properties field with Standard and Custom modes for flexible property configuration and editing * Introduced multi-value property support for advanced parameter management * **Refactoring** * Improved field factory logic with dedicated schema-matching helpers for better maintainability * **Tests** * Added comprehensive test coverage for new endpoint properties and multi-value editing functionality * **Style** * Added styling for endpoint properties field UI components

Step 2/3: Extract type-related logic from XmlSchemaDocumentUtilService and JsonSchemaDocumentUtilService into dedicated XmlSchemaTypesService and JsonSchemaTypesService classes, providing better separation of concerns between type operations and document operations.

  - Create XmlSchemaTypesService with methods moved from XmlSchemaDocumentUtilService: parseTypeOverride, mapTypeStringToEnum, determineOverrideVariant, and type inheritance checks (isExtensionOrRestriction, checkComplexTypeInheritance, checkSimpleTypeInheritance)
  - Add new XmlSchemaTypesService methods for type querying: getAllUserDefinedTypes, getAllXmlSchemaTypes, getAllBuiltInTypes, getExtensionsAndRestrictions, getTypeOverrideCandidatesForField
  - Create JsonSchemaTypesService with methods moved from JsonSchemaDocumentUtilService: parseTypeOverride, mapTypeStringToEnum, determineOverrideVariant
  - Add new JsonSchemaTypesService methods: getAllJsonSchemaTypes, getTypeOverrideCandidatesForField

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2724

Step 1/3: Store DocumentDefinition on IDocument and simplify document construction

While implementing Field Type Override API, it was found that more parameters held in DocumentDefinition are needed to be delivered. Refactor document model to accept and retain the full DocumentDefinition in constructors instead of individual documentType/documentId parameters and keep holding it. This makes DocumentDefinition the single source of truth for document creation as well as dynamic modification like Field Type Override, and allows downstream code to access the original definition.

  - Add `definition` property to IDocument/BaseDocument
  - Change BaseDocument, PrimitiveDocument, JsonSchemaDocument, and XmlSchemaDocument constructors to accept DocumentDefinition
  - Make DocumentDefinition.name required (was optional)
  - Remove redundant document ID derivation logic from services
  - Fix bug in DocumentService where `if (document)` should be `if (result.document)`
  - Update all tests and stubs to match new constructor API
pull_requests_reviewed.title: feat(DataMapper): Implement API to manage overrides 1/3

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Added an icon button to direct endpoints on the canvas which acts as a "Go To" functionality. When clicking the button on a To node the selection changes to the referenced direct from endpoint. If the route containing this element is not visible, it will be made visible. There is also the reverse functionality where you can jump from a direct from endpoint to the caller. If there are multiple callers then a popover is shown which lets the user select the right route. 

- Add direct endpoint normalization for both URI notations:
  - `uri: direct` + `parameters.name`
  - `uri: direct:<name>` (including query params)
- Resolve direct links from both route and REST callers
- Add reverse navigation from `from(direct:...)` back to caller(s)
- Show a route chooser popover when multiple callers exist
- Keep single-caller behavior as one-click navigation
- Hide popover close icon and improve chooser UX (button-style entries, plain list)
- Fix stale navigation icon updates after source changes
- Extend unit tests for URI parsing and direct route navigation flows

Fixes #1613
Fixes #1487

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, copy & paste functionality for Canvas entities, requires allowing access to the clipboard right away when loading Kaoto. In addition to that, since this process happens too early in the chain, the result is always false, blocking the functionality.

The fix is to delay accessing the clipboard until the context menu is opened, this way, we get fresh results every time.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2728 --- # UI Mockup for xs:choice Improvement (1st attempt) ## Overview This PR provides interactive UI mockups for the xs:choice feature implementation (#2358, #2728). These Storybook stories demonstrate the complete user experience including choice visualization, selection workflow, nested choices with smart numbering, conditional mapping, and error handling in the DataMapper. ## Mockup Stories This PR includes 4 interactive Storybook stories that demonstrate different aspects of the xs:choice feature: ---
1. Main Story Complete Interactive Workflow **Purpose:** Primary demonstration of the entire choice workflow in a Person document. #### Interactive workflow * Select option: Right-click the `choice` control node to open the context menu, then click `Selecti option` * Revert option: right-click the node selected from `choice` to open the context menu, then click `Revert to choice` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17ae5e7e-f595-47d4-a7f7-9bda08d4705d #### Standard choice `choice (email | phone | address)` with 3 options * Expanded * Collapsed #### Truncated choice `choice (email | sms | push ... +5 more)` with 8 options showing truncation * Expanded * Collapsed #### Collection choice `preferredContactMethods` with `maxOccurs > 1` showing layer icon * Expanded * Collapsed #### Right-click context menu #### Selection dialog Radio button list with type and description for each option * Dialog * Dialog (maxOccurs > 1) #### Revert functionality Context menu to restore choice node
---
2. Nested Choices Direct Nested Choice Scenario **Purpose:** Demonstrates direct nested choices with smart numbering logic. **Numbering Rules:** - **Multiple choice members** → `choice1`, `choice2`, etc. - **Single choice member** → `choice` (no number) #### Smart numbering Multiple choice members numbered as `choice1`, `choice2`, etc. #### Multi-level revert Revert child choice first, then parent choice https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b99c171-c8df-4816-a4b7-0122bb5109b4
---
3. Conditional Mapping Result - choose/when/otherwise When users drag the source `choice` node onto a target field, it automatically creates choose-when-otherwise conditional mapping, with `when` branches for each choice member as well as an otherwise branch. **Purpose:** Shows the result when a source choice is dragged to a target field, creating XSLT conditional mapping structure. * Auto-generated `choose/when/otherwise` structure * One `when` branch per choice member with XPath test expressions * `otherwise` branch for fallback
---
4. Choice to Choice Error - Validation Error Case It's not allowed to drag choice node and drop onto the other side of choice node. It disables the droppable area highlight and show an error message if it's performed. **Purpose:** Demonstrates error handling when users attempt invalid choice-to-choice mapping. #### Dismissible error alert #### Future Enhancement Note Selection dialog to map each source option to specific target option upfront (https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2763), which eventually allows choice-to-choice drag and drop

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
XML attributes with complex type references (e.g., FHIR XSD where attributes extend from Element) were showing expand buttons because DocumentService.hasChildren() only checked for child fields or type references, without verifying if the field was an attribute. In XML Schema, attributes are always atomic and cannot have children.


Added check in DocumentService.hasChildren() to return false for attributes regardless of type references.



Fixes #2640

feat(SourceCode): Sort properties using camel catalog

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, YAML properties are sorted alphabetically. For Camel Routes, the order is based on hardcoded priorities for `OnException`, `ErrorHandler`, etc.

The problem with that approach is that the order is only used when adding new entities to the Camel Resource, and when serializing back, the order is different, therefore causing runtime exceptions when launching the route.

### Changes
The fix is to remove the need to sort the entities when serializing, by placing them in the right position from the beginning. In addition to that, sorting the properties using the Camel Catalog to have a consistent diff between edits.

fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2833
fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/1577

Replaces the Card-based DataMapper panels with a new ExpansionPanels component. Main advantage is better navigation when multiple schemas are attached across multiple parameters and the source body.  

Component provides:

  - Resizable panels - Drag the handle between panels to resize, with proportional space redistribution
  - Collapsible sections - Click panel headers to expand/collapse
  - Container-aware layout - Automatically adapts when the container is resized (e.g., drawer open/close)
  - Real-time mapping line updates and clamping

  Architecture changes
  - Simplified panel structure with CSS Grid layout instead of nested Cards
  - Document headers and content now managed by ExpansionPanel, reducing component complexity

  Migration scope

  - Source panel (Parameters + Body sections)
  - Target panel (Body section)
  - All existing DataMapper functionality preserved




https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1aa19609-8ec8-4efa-a518-66436550d548




related to: #2470
fixes [#2264](https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2264)

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Fixed incorrect expand icon shown for XML attribute fields in DataMapper tree

This PR fixes an issue where XML attribute fields (for example, @value) were incorrectly rendered with an expand/collapse icon in the DataMapper document tree, primarily on the target side when using certain XSDs.

The root cause was that expandability was derived solely from hasChildren, which in some cases was temporarily true for attribute nodes during schema initialization.

Closes : https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2640
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fixed expand button on attributes

pull_requests_reviewed.body: Fixes: #2776
pull_requests_reviewed.title: fix(xmlExpressionParser): Correctly parse beans

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Refactored the IField interface by separating core, type-related, and choice-related properties into dedicated interfaces and composing them back together. This enables adding choice metadata without breaking existing code, improves interface clarity, and keeps runtime behavior unchanged.

Issue : https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2729
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Refactor IField to support choice metadata with cleaner interface structure

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes #2492 

Please test the changes here: https://shivamg640.github.io/kaoto/
Simply add a Choice EIP to the route and try duplicating the When container. Earlier, this action resulted in incorrectly ordered Choice visualization, which is now fixed.

This change enables the Disable action for DataMapper steps in the node context menu. DataMapper is a synthetic Kaoto construct and is not backed by the Camel processor schema, so it previously could not expose the Disable capability, which is derived from schema metadata for standard EIPs.

To address this, the Disable capability is explicitly enabled for DataMapper in the capability check, aligning its behavior with other single-step processors. 

FIxes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2546

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Fixes: #2770 

Changes introduced:
1. Added drop support on edges by making edges static while dragging associated nodes.
2. Restricted drop support on step nodes (collapsed When containers can still be dragged and dropped onto other When containers).
3. Introduced a static ghost node in place of the dragged node to fill the gap between edges during drag operations.
4. Added placeholders as the last node in every step, removing the floating quick append-step icon from the final node.
5. Reintroduced Move Before/After buttons in step toolbar.
6. Made drop targets coloring consistent for edge and placeholder nodes.

Above changes can be tested here: https://shivamg640.github.io/kaoto/

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
…a files

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2727

Support multiple schema files referencing from one to another, for both XML and JSON. While xml-schema-ts already has a capability to organize multiple schema files, we needed to add `JsonSchemaCollection` class to represent a collection of multiple JSON schema files. Also `JsonSchemaReferece` class is introduced to represent the JSON schema `$ref`.

In order to encapsulate the handling of root element (XML) and primary schema (JSON) into `XmlSchemaDocumentService` and `JsonSchemaDocumentService`, now
- `XmlSchemaDocumentService.createXmlSchemaDocument()` returns `CreateXmlSchemaDocumentResult`
- `JsonSchemaDocumentService.createJsonSchemaDocument()` returns `CreateJsonSchemaDocumentResult`

Renamed model files, they were splitted from service but is no longer a service but a collection of model classes
- `xml-schema-document-model.service.ts` to `xml-schema-document.model.ts`
- `json-schema-document-model.service.ts` to `json-schema-document.model.ts`

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### **Added Layout specific Icons according to canvas layout**

This PR enhances the node context menu by making its action icons layout-aware. The Prepend and Append actions now use single-arrow icons, while Move Before and Move Next use double-arrow icons. All these icons dynamically adapt to the current canvas layout (horizontal or vertical), ensuring the direction of each action is visually clear and consistent with the flow orientation. This change improves usability without altering any existing behavior or functionality.

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2424

### Context
Currently, we're setting the reference to the mapping links area using a `useState` hook to store it.

### Changes
Since this rerender is not necessary, we can skip the `useState` hook and use a `useRef` directly instead. This simplifies the `MappingLinksContext`'s API.

This is also a prerequisite of updating React to v19.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
**Preserve Camel Expression Placeholders During URI Serialization**

This PR fixes an issue where expression-based parameter values (e.g. {{demo.period}} or ${header.foo}) were being URL-encoded during Camel URI serialization. This caused placeholder values to be converted into their encoded forms (e.g. %7B%7Bdemo.period%7D%7D), leading to broken behavior when switching between Form and Source views or after refreshing the editor.

**Changes Made**

- Introduced a new expression detection utility
- Updated URI query parameter serialization logic to skip URL encoding for expressions
- Ensured literal values (e.g. those containing spaces) continue to be properly encoded


[Screencast from 2025-12-06 19-01-17.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/833acb8b-de59-4fdf-935c-b08d91af3d00)

Fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2719

### Fix: Empty Choice EIP Rendering in Flow Diagram / Distortion in the UI after removing child of choice EIP 

**This PR fixes a layout issue where removing all sub-steps (when / otherwise) from a Choice step caused:**

- The Choice EIP to incorrectly remain marked as group
- The UI to distort and render the step at the top-left corner of the canvas

**After fixes** 
 
-  Empty Choice nodes remain in correct order
-  No UI layout corruption after deleting children
-  No duplicate edges
-  Consistent and predictable flow graph structure

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Context
The `FlowExportImage` component previously exported the graph by manipulating the **visible canvas** (`reset`, `fit`, `layout`) before calling `toPng()`. While functional, this caused a brief **flicker** as the canvas moved during the export process.

Ticket #2748 requested recreating the canvas in an **invisible component** to prevent this flicker and improve the user experience.

## Solution
- Instead of creating a new `VisualizationController` instance (which is not publicly exposed), we **clone the visible canvas DOM node** into an off-screen container.
- The cloned container is rendered **completely off-screen and invisible**:
  - Positioned at `top: -99999px, left: -99999px`
  - Fully visible to the export (`opacity: 1`) but off-screen
  - Pointer events disabled
- Export is performed using `html-to-image` on the cloned DOM node.
- The PNG download is triggered automatically after the export.
- A **spinner overlay** (`isExporting`) is displayed while the export is in progress.

This approach ensures:
- The visible canvas remains untouched → **no flicker**
- Full export of the graph without cutting off any parts
- No TypeScript errors related to internal controller methods
- Full compatibility with `useVisualizationController()`

## Results
- Clicking the export button downloads the PNG without affecting the visible graph.
- The spinner overlay provides user feedback during export.
- The exported image accurately represents the current graph state.

## Testing
- Verified export button triggers the spinner and downloads a PNG file.
- Verified the visible canvas does **not move** during export.
- Verified the exported PNG matches the visible graph.
- Verified multiple exports work consecutively without errors.

Fixes #2750
pull_requests_reviewed.title: fix(ui): implement flicker-free export for FlowExportImage

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Replace `ADD` with `COPY` in `Dockerfile`

From SonarCloud:
> Using the ADD instruction instead of COPY for local resources in Dockerfiles can lead to several issues, including unexpected behavior, increased complexity, and security risks. The ADD instruction has additional features that can introduce unintended side effects, such as automatically extracting compressed files and fetching remote resources. This can make the behavior of the instruction less predictable and harder to understand or even lead to security issues, if, for example, due to a typo in the source path, the ADD instruction could fetch a remote resource instead of copying a local file.

> If you only need to copy local files or directories into your Docker image, it is recommended to use the COPY instruction instead. Only use the ADD instruction when you need its additional features, such as fetching remote resources or extracting compressed files. See also the rule S7026 for more information on using the ADD instruction to fetch remote resources.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Export as image is exporting only the visible part of the canvas instead of the full diagram #2526

This PR fixes the issue where exporting the canvas as an image only captured the currently visible (zoomed) portion instead of the full diagram.

**What’s Fixed**
- The export now resets and fits the full topology graph before capturing.
- A dark loading overlay is shown during export so the zoom reset is never visible to the user.
- Exported PNG always contains the complete diagram, even if parts were off-screen.

After Fix 
[Screencast from 2025-11-25 22-53-04.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/64d7a0d9-fda5-4502-b7bf-59d26fcad276)

Fix: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2526

pull_requests_reviewed.body: ### Support "wrap with if" on a field which already has a mapping #1861 This PR adds support for applying Wrap with IF (and other conditional wrappers) on target fields that already have an existing mapping. **Problem Before** - When a field had an active mapping (e.g. /Person/Name), the actions menu disappeared. - Only edit and delete icons were shown. - Users could not wrap an existing mapping with: - if - choose/when/otherwise - selector expressions **Fix Implemented** - The actions menu (3-dot menu) is now visible for mapped fields as well. - Added support for executing: - Wrap with IF - when and otherwise - Ensures mapped fields behave the same as unmapped fields regarding transformation actions. Before After

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, our CamelCatalogService has a synchronous API and doesn't support fetching data from remote endpoints.

This commit introduces a new `DynamicCatalog` with an `async` API and featuring a pluggable providers concept.

This architectural change allows to have different catalogs from many different sources, like remote Kamelets, Camel Forage bits, etc.

relates: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2734

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, hand-made mocks are used in the `Forms` stories, causing that whenever there's an `API` change in the `IVisualizationNode`, the mocks need to be updated accordingly.

### Changes
This commit consolidates the forms-related stories into a single file and uses a real Camel Route Visual entity to reduce the amount of mocks needed, positioning the stories closer to a runtime usage.

From now on, when a new EIP or component needs to be showcased through Storybook, it's as simple as adding a new step in the stub route and creating a new template for it.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: chore(Storybook): Consolidate Forms stories
chore: release 2.8.2

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
The `VisualComponentSchema` interface represents the schema and the node definition, and it's one of the first introduced elements in Kaoto.

Since we didn't perform many actions with the node definitions, having them together was convenient. Nowadays, we interact with the node definition a lot more than before, so this combination becomes problematic. (copy/paste, node label, description, processing its IDs, duplicate, etc)

This commit splits the `getComponentSchema()` method at the
`IVisualizationNode` level in 2 dedicated
methods:
```
getNodeSchema(): KaotoSchemaDefinition['schema'] | undefined;
getNodeDefinition(): unknown;
```

At this stage, the `VisualizationNode` class still calls the underlying
`getComponentSchema` method from the `BaseEntity` as a means to
iteratively perform the split.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: chore: Split `getComponentSchema()` into dedicated methods

Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2545

Following up https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/pull/2653

### In VS Code
Duplicate the metadata reference (e.g. schema) and XSLT contents, but copy the XSLT file contents into a different file so that the mappings won't mix

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af291fd7-2f6f-4cfb-85bb-47d511068bfa

### Browser
Since ATM we don't have external file handling capability in the browser, we just copy&paste/duplicate the step in YAML, but clear the XSLT file URI. Once we implement the MetadataContext for browser, it will automatically get to work for copy/paste/duplicate.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a3e5ef14-d75e-474c-9b67-3d2aca719987

- fix(Select): improve type consistency in `onSelect` by [@​logonoff](https://redirect.github.com/logonoff) in [#​11935](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/11935)
- fix(Truncate): Update truncate examples to use PF6 tokens to match core by [@​tlabaj](https://redirect.github.com/tlabaj) in [#​11943](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/11943)
- chore(table): remove card that wraps table content by [@​mcoker](https://redirect.github.com/mcoker) in [#​11959](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/11959)
- chore(docs): Update docs to suggest using preventOverflow by [@​rebeccaalpert](https://redirect.github.com/rebeccaalpert) in [#​12012](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12012)
- feat(CodeEditor): use custom PatternFly monaco theme by [@​logonoff](https://redirect.github.com/logonoff) in [#​11785](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/11785)
- fix(MenuGroup): fix title generation when the string is empty by [@​rohit-bharmal](https://redirect.github.com/rohit-bharmal) in [#​11999](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/11999)
- chore: bump core and docs-framework by [@​mcoker](https://redirect.github.com/mcoker) in [#​12016](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12016)
- feat(Table): allow extra content to be appended to th by [@​kmcfaul](https://redirect.github.com/kmcfaul) in [#​12017](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12017)
- fix: Bumped echarts and core. by [@​dlabaj](https://redirect.github.com/dlabaj) in [#​12023](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12023)
- chore: Added back documentation upload by [@​dlabaj](https://redirect.github.com/dlabaj) in [#​12032](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12032)
- chore(alert): put demo buttons into flex so they wrap by [@​srambach](https://redirect.github.com/srambach) in [#​12020](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12020)
- fix(examples): make sure examples with SearchInput have onClear by [@​gitdallas](https://redirect.github.com/gitdallas) in [#​12044](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12044)
- feat(templates): allow react 19 by [@​kmcfaul](https://redirect.github.com/kmcfaul) in [#​12046](https://redirect.github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react/pull/12046)

##### New Contributors

pull_requests_reviewed.body: ## Description Currently, the DataMapper UI allows users to drag and drop a **primitive source Body** (without an attached schema) and create a mapping. This is invalid at runtime because **camel-xslt-saxon** expects the incoming message body to be an XML Document (or null if `failOnNullBody=false`). Fixes: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2637 This PR fixes the issue by: - Making **primitive source Body** not draggable/droppable in the UI. - Ensuring **Parameters** remain draggable/droppable even if primitive (they are valid). - Maintaining overall drag-and-drop functionality and UI stability. ## Screenshot *Screenshot showing that primitive Body cannot be dragged while Parameters can.* ## Steps Taken / Testing 1. Attempted to drag primitive Body → drag is blocked; mapping cannot be created. 2. Dragged Parameters → mapping works as expected. 3. Verified console → no errors or warnings appear. 4. Checked other drag-and-drop actions → no regressions observed. 5. Confirmed expected runtime behavior: only valid mappings can be created. ## Additional Notes - Optional UX improvement: visual cue (greyed out Body / `not-allowed` cursor) can be added later. - This ensures users cannot create mappings that would fail at runtime. Fixes #2637
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Fix: Prevent mapping of primitive Body without schema

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, the `SourceDocumentNode` component contains diverse logic for renaming parameters, setting schemas, and more, as this component is used for the Schema header, node rows, and parameters.

### Changes
This PR extracts each domain logic into dedicated components. For instance, the rename parameter functionality was moved to a dedicated `ParameterDocument` component; this way, it's easier to reason about the component's responsibilities.

### Note
This is a prerequisite for introducing virtual scrolling.

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
Created shared Document component that is reused by:
- SourceDocument (static "Body" title)
- ParamDocument (editable title with rename/delete actions)
- TargetDocument (static "Body" title, TODO: add XPath input)

Key changes:
- Document.tsx: Pure composition container with ReactNode title prop
- Supports expand/collapse for all document types
- AttachSchema/DetachSchema buttons use correct document name
- Parameter rename working with proper state management
- Input styling fixed (min-width for visibility)

TODO:
- Add XPath input to TargetDocument for primitive body mappings
- Consider performance optimization with React.memo when refactoring child nodes

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
### Context
Currently, the `Truncate` component from Patternfly seems to be passing a `ref` property into the underlying DOM nodes, and this is causing an error to be thrown in the browser's console.

### Changes
A workaround for this situation is to remove the `Truncate` component and replace it with a simple `span` DOM node instead.
pull_requests_reviewed.title: chore: Remove innerRef warning when loading Datamapper

pull_requests_reviewed.body: ### Context Data mapper connection lines were extending outside their intended container boundaries, creating visual confusion when source or target elements were scrolled out of view. ### Changes - Implemented 6 distinct clipping scenarios: 1. Source visible → target above: curve to top center 2. Source visible → target below: curve to bottom center 3. Source above → target visible: curve from top center 4. Source below → target visible: curve from bottom center 5. Both not visible: straight line top to bottom 6. Both visible: unchanged behavior ### Screenshot ### Notes Continuation of: https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/pull/2464

fixes #2610 

Now Attaching the target as JSON schema with no mappings doesn't add the <xsl:value-of select="xml-to-json()"/> entry to the XSLT. However When deleting all the mapping, we again see <xsl:value-of select="xml-to-json()"/> entry in the XSLT. This will get automatically fixed once we delete the unnecessary mapping children when no mappings exist (https://github.com/KaotoIO/kaoto/issues/2618).
